qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL Playground embeds masterKey in client-side JavaScript HTML response
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes masterKey exposure in GraphQL Playground HTML responses. The advisory describes JWT algorithm confusion in the Google auth adapter allowing authentication bypass. These are completely different vulnerabilities in different components with different root causes.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: allowPublicExplain defaults to true exposing MongoDB query plans to unauthenticated users
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes MongoDB explain output being exposed to unauthenticated users via the allowPublicExplain default. The advisory describes JWT algorithm confusion in the Google auth adapter. These are unrelated vulnerabilities with different vulnerable code paths and different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the two qualifying vulnerabilities match the advisory-described JWT algorithm confusion vulnerability in the Google auth adapter. The advisory describes an authentication bypass vulnerability where an attacker can forge Google authentication tokens using the 'none' algorithm to log in as any user. The two qualifying findings describe completely different vulnerabilities: (1) masterKey exposure in GraphQL Playground HTML, and (2) MongoDB explain output exposure via allowPublicExplain default. Neither finding relates to JWT validation, Google authentication, or the specific vulnerable code paths mentioned in the advisory patches.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-4q3h-vp4r-prv2 describes JWT algorithm confusion in Google auth adapter (CVE-2026-27804)
Finding 1: masterKey exposure in GraphQL Playground - unrelated to JWT/Google auth
Finding 2: MongoDB explain output exposure - unrelated to JWT/Google auth
No finding references GoogleAdapter, JWT algorithm validation, or authentication bypass via forged tokens
Advisory patches reference commits to Google auth adapter and JWT validation code, none of which are mentioned in any finding
